basic_details.job_title: SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026: 67 Paramedical and Non-Paramedical Vacancies, Apply Online from 15 July
basic_details.organization_name: Sri Venkateswara Institute of Medical Sciences (SVIMS), TTD, Tirupati
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Computer Based Test (or weightage method if fewer than 10 applicants), Experience, Interview
basic_details.short_information: Sri Venkateswara Institute of Medical Sciences (SVIMS), Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams, Tirupati has released two separate recruitment notifications dated 13 July 2026, together inviting applications for 67 vacancies across Paramedical and Non-Paramedical posts. Online applications open on 15 July 2026 and close on 10 August 2026 (up to 11:59 p.m.) at svimstpt.ap.nic.in.

important_dates.correction_date: Not specified - no corrections are entertained through email/letter after online submission
important_dates.computer based test date: Not yet released - to be intimated through email/SMS
important_dates.skill test date (steno cum junior assist: Not yet released - to be announced on the official website

application_fee.details: Fee must be paid through the online payment gateway (UPI, Credit Card, Debit Card, or Net Banking) provided on the application portal. No other mode is accepted. Fee once remitted is not refundable under any circumstances, including rejection of application or cancellation of the notification. Separate fee is payable for each category/post applied for.

age_limit.details: Candidates must have completed 18 years and must not have completed 42 years of age as on 01-07-2026 (as per G.O.Ms.No.109, GAD (Service-A) Dept., dated 08-10-2025). Notification 03: Age relaxation of 5 years applies to SC, ST, BC, and EWS candidates. Notification 02: Age relaxation of 5 years applies to SC, ST, and BC candidates (EWS is not separately mentioned in this notification's age-relaxation clause). Persons presently working in SVIMS on Adhoc/Outsourcing/Contract basis get a 5-year relaxation, subject to completing a minimum of 5 years of service in SVIMS as on the date of notification. The maximum age limit with all relaxations put together is 52 years.

qualification.details: Notification 02: Open only to candidates native to the State of Andhra Pradesh (post bifurcation), who must upload original study certificates for 4 academic years (7th to 10th class), or a residence certificate from the concerned Tahsildar as per Appendix-I of the notification, if they did not study in any institution during that period. Notification 03: Open only to candidates native to Tirupati District (post bifurcation of the districts). Nativity must be established via study in an educational institution in the local area for at least 4 consecutive academic years ending with the qualifying exam year, or 4 years' residence in the local area if not studying during that period. Both notifications require candidates to profess the Hindu religion, per TTD Employees Service Rules, 1989. Reservation for SC, ST, BC, Women, and (per notification) EWS follows Rule 22 and 22-A of the Andhra Pradesh State Subordinate Service Rules, as applicable to TTD institutions under G.O.Ms.No.1372, dated 25-10-2007. Candidates claiming BC (A/B/D) reservation must upload the latest community certificate; failure to do so means the candidature is considered under Open Category only. EWS applicants (where applicable) must submit an EWS certificate issued on or after 1 April 2026, confirming gross annual family income for FY 2025-26 below Rs. 8,00,000/-. A person in a bigamous marriage is not eligible. Applicants must not have been convicted by any court of law.

salary_details.details: Note: These are RPS 2022 pay-scale ranges as stated in the notification. In-hand monthly salary figures are not specified in the source document.

selection_process: Written Test (CBT) 75 75 multiple-choice questions, 1 mark each, 90-minute duration, no negative marking
Experience 15 1 mark per completed 6-month block of relevant service (max 15 marks), preferably in Govt./Semi-Govt./PSU institutions or hospitals with 500+ beds meeting EPF/ESI statutory compliance
Interview 10 Final stage after CBT and experience evaluation
split as follows for both notifications:
If fewer than 10 applications are received for a post
selection is done via weightage of qualifying-examination marks
and interview instead of a CBT. If more than 10 applications are received
a Computer Based Test is conducted through AP Online Service at various test centres. Minimum qualifying marks in the CBT: Notification 02 - OC 40%
SC/ST 30%. Steno Cum Junior Assistant (Notification 03 only): Candidates who clear the CBT qualifying marks (35% BC
30% SC/ST as stated for this stage) are eligible for a separate Skill Test worth 50 marks
on payment of Rs. 590/- fee. Final selection for this post uses a weightage of 50 marks from the CBT and 25 marks from the Skill Test

per AP State Board of Technical Education norms. The question paper for the CBT will be in English. Normalization formula is applied when an exam for the same post is conducted in multiple shifts/sessions; marks are calculated up to 5 decimal places. In case of a tie in marks
the elder candidate is given preference; if age also ties
the candidate with more experience is preferred. The wait list is maintained for one year from the date of provisional selection.
how_to_apply: Visit the official SVIMS website: https://svimstpt.ap.nic.in
Go to the "Jobs" tab and locate the relevant notification - No.02/Para & Non Para/2026/Estt. or No.03/Non Para/2026/Estt., as applicable to your eligibility.
Register and fill in the online application form with personal, educational, and category details.
Upload all required scanned documents as per the checklist in the notification.
Pay the prescribed application/examination fee through the online payment gateway (UPI/Credit Card/Debit Card/Net Banking).
Review all entered details carefully, since no corrections are allowed after submission.
Submit the application before 10 August 2026, 11:59 p.m.
Save/download the submitted application for future reference.
The application window is open from 15 July 2026 to 10 August 2026.
important_instructions: SVIMS reserves the right to cancel the recruitment notification at any stage and to fill or not fill any or all posts. Notified vacancies are provisional and subject to variation at the discretion of the Director-cum-VC, SVIMS. Candidates may choose a test centre with three preferences, but AP Online Services reserves the right to allot any centre; requests for centre change will not be entertained. Mere participation in the exam or securing qualifying marks does not confer any right to selection or appointment; selection is provisional and subject to verification of original certificates, community/local status, experience, antecedents, and medical fitness. Submission of false certificates or information can lead to cancellation of candidature/appointment and criminal action. No TA/DA is paid for attending the written test or interview. Use of mobile phones (even switched off), calculators, smart watches, Bluetooth devices, or other electronic gadgets is strictly prohibited at the exam venue. All disputes are subject to the jurisdiction of the Court of Law, Andhra Pradesh.

content_sections.heading: SVIMS Tirupati Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: Notification 02 covers 19 Paramedical vacancies and 15 Non-Paramedical vacancies, for a total of 34 posts. Notification 03 covers Junior Assistant Cadre and allied posts, totalling 33 vacancies. Note: the source table uses BC sub-columns A, B, D and SC Group I, III columns; the combined figures above are consolidated from those columns for readability. Refer to Part 1 CMS table or the official notification for the exact column-wise breakup.

content_sections.heading: SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026: Educational Qualification
content_sections.text: Clinical Psychologist: Master's degree in Psychology (UGC-recognized university) with 2 years' experience in Neurology or Psychiatry in a reputed hospital. Desirable: M.Sc. in Clinical Psychology. Bio Medical Engineer: B.Tech in Bio-Medical Engineering (AICTE-recognized) with 3 years' experience in a corporate hospital or medical-equipment MNC. Instrument Mechanic Gr-II: Diploma in Bio-Medical Engineering or equivalent (Govt. of AP recognized) with 2 years' experience in a reputed corporate hospital. Nuclear Medicine Technologist Gr-I: M.Sc./B.Sc. with PG Diploma in Medical Radio Isotope Technology (DMRIT) from BARC or equivalent, or M.Sc. in Nuclear Medicine Technology, or M.Sc. MRIT from a recognized institute. Several desirable qualifications listed in the notification (see official PDF). Nuclear Medicine Technologist Gr-II: B.Sc. with 2-year PG Diploma in Nuclear Medicine Technology (AERB-recognized institution), OR B.Sc. with 1-year Certificate Course in Radiographic Assistant (CRA) plus 4 years' experience. Must be registered with the Andhra Pradesh Paramedical Board (APPMB). Speech Therapist: B.Sc. in Speech Therapy and Audiology with 2 years' experience in a reputed hospital. Desirable: M.Sc. in Speech Pathology. Perfusion Technologist Gr-II / Assistant Perfusionist: B.Sc. plus 2-year PG Diploma in Cardio-Pulmonary Perfusion Technology, APPMB registration required. Pharmacist Gr-II: Bachelor's degree in Pharmacy (AICTE-recognized) with Pharmacy Council of India registration, OR Diploma in Pharmacy (AP Board of Technical Education) with PCI registration and 3 years' experience in a reputed corporate hospital. Audiometry Technician: Bachelor's degree in Speech, Hearing & Language Pathology / Diploma in Audiometry Technician, with RCI or AP Paramedical Board registration. Medical Communication & Publicity Officer: Graduate degree in Fine Arts (JNTU Hyderabad or institution of repute) with specialized training in advertising/publicity/videography, plus 3 years' experience. Assistant Accounts Officer: Postgraduate in Commerce with CA-Inter and at least 5 years' relevant experience, OR a Chartered Accountant. Assistant Librarian: Post-graduation in Library Information Sciences (MLISc). M.Phil./Ph.D. preferred. Networking Engineer: B.Tech (CSE) / MS with network-specialization experience. Assistant Public Relation Officer: M.A./M.Sc. in Arts or Social Sciences plus a Degree or Diploma in Journalism or Public Relations. Photographer: Intermediate examination pass plus Diploma/Certificate in Photography, with 2 years' experience in a similar role. Health Inspector: Degree from a recognized university plus Sanitary Inspector Training Course certificate, with 5 years' experience in housekeeping in a public sector or reputed organization. Electrician: SSC pass plus DEE or ITI Electrical Trade Certificate, with 3 years' experience in a reputed organization. Junior Assistant / Cashier / Billing Clerk: Bachelor's degree with knowledge of computer applic

content_sections.heading: SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026: Age Limit
content_sections.text: Candidates must have completed 18 years and must not have completed 42 years of age as on 01-07-2026 (as per G.O.Ms.No.109, GAD (Service-A) Dept., dated 08-10-2025). Notification 03: Age relaxation of 5 years applies to SC, ST, BC, and EWS candidates. Notification 02: Age relaxation of 5 years applies to SC, ST, and BC candidates (EWS is not separately mentioned in this notification's age-relaxation clause). Persons presently working in SVIMS on Adhoc/Outsourcing/Contract basis get a 5-year relaxation, subject to completing a minimum of 5 years of service in SVIMS as on the date of notification. The maximum age limit with all relaxations put together is 52 years.

content_sections.heading: SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Fee must be paid through the online payment gateway (UPI, Credit Card, Debit Card, or Net Banking) provided on the application portal. No other mode is accepted. Fee once remitted is not refundable under any circumstances, including rejection of application or cancellation of the notification. Separate fee is payable for each category/post applied for.

rows.notification: 03 (Steno Cum Junior Assistant Skill Test only)

content_sections.heading: SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Total marks: 100, split as follows for both notifications: If fewer than 10 applications are received for a post, selection is done via weightage of qualifying-examination marks, experience, and interview instead of a CBT. If more than 10 applications are received, a Computer Based Test is conducted through AP Online Service at various test centres. Minimum qualifying marks in the CBT: Notification 02 - OC 40%, BC 35%, SC/ST 30%. Notification 03 - OC/EWS 40%, BC 35%, SC/ST 30%. Steno Cum Junior Assistant (Notification 03 only): Candidates who clear the CBT qualifying marks (35% BC, 30% SC/ST as stated for this stage) are eligible for a separate Skill Test worth 50 marks, on payment of Rs. 590/- fee. Final selection for this post uses a weightage of 50 marks from the CBT and 25 marks from the Skill Test, per AP State Board of Technical Education norms. The question paper for the CBT will be in English. Normalization formula is applied when an exam for the same post is conducted in multiple shifts/sessions; marks are calculated up to 5 decimal places. In case of a tie in marks, the elder candidate is given preference; if age also ties, the candidate with more experience is preferred. The wait list is maintained for one year from the date of provisional selection.

rows.details: 75 multiple-choice questions, 1 mark each, 90-minute duration, no negative marking
1 mark per completed 6-month block of relevant service (max 15 marks), preferably in Govt./Semi-Govt./PSU institutions or hospitals with 500+ beds meeting EPF/ESI statutory compliance
Final stage after CBT and experience evaluation

content_sections.heading: SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026: Eligibility & Reservation
content_sections.text: Notification 02: Open only to candidates native to the State of Andhra Pradesh (post bifurcation), who must upload original study certificates for 4 academic years (7th to 10th class), or a residence certificate from the concerned Tahsildar as per Appendix-I of the notification, if they did not study in any institution during that period. Notification 03: Open only to candidates native to Tirupati District (post bifurcation of the districts). Nativity must be established via study in an educational institution in the local area for at least 4 consecutive academic years ending with the qualifying exam year, or 4 years' residence in the local area if not studying during that period. Both notifications require candidates to profess the Hindu religion, per TTD Employees Service Rules, 1989. Reservation for SC, ST, BC, Women, and (per notification) EWS follows Rule 22 and 22-A of the Andhra Pradesh State Subordinate Service Rules, as applicable to TTD institutions under G.O.Ms.No.1372, dated 25-10-2007. Candidates claiming BC (A/B/D) reservation must upload the latest community certificate; failure to do so means the candidature is considered under Open Category only. EWS applicants (where applicable) must submit an EWS certificate issued on or after 1 April 2026, confirming gross annual family income for FY 2025-26 below Rs. 8,00,000/-. A person in a bigamous marriage is not eligible. Applicants must not have been convicted by any court of law.

content_sections.heading: SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026: Documents Required
content_sections.text: Date of birth proof / SSC certificate Educational qualification certificates and marks memos Latest community certificate (SC/ST/BC), where reservation is claimed EWS certificate (issued on/after 01-04-2026), where applicable - Notification 03 only Study/residence/nativity certificate from the concerned Tahsildar Registration certificate, wherever prescribed (e.g., AP Paramedical Board Registration for Paramedical posts; AP Pharmacy Council Registration for Pharmacist Gr-II) Experience certificate(s) from the Head(s) of Institution/Organization, with duration and pay details, along with EPF statements for the last 2 years Appointment/engagement order for previous service, salary proof/pay slips, EPF/ESI statements (wherever applicable) No Objection Certificate from the employer, if currently employed in a Government department Any other certificate prescribed for the specific post applied for All documents must be uploaded as clear scanned copies at the time of online application submission. No corrections are entertained after submission through any mode.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the official SVIMS website: https://svimstpt.ap.nic.in Go to the "Jobs" tab and locate the relevant notification - No.02/Para & Non Para/2026/Estt. or No.03/Non Para/2026/Estt., as applicable to your eligibility. Register and fill in the online application form with personal, educational, and category details. Upload all required scanned documents as per the checklist in the notification. Pay the prescribed application/examination fee through the online payment gateway (UPI/Credit Card/Debit Card/Net Banking). Review all entered details carefully, since no corrections are allowed after submission. Submit the application before 10 August 2026, 11:59 p.m. Save/download the submitted application for future reference. The application window is open from 15 July 2026 to 10 August 2026.

content_sections.heading: SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: SVIMS reserves the right to cancel the recruitment notification at any stage and to fill or not fill any or all posts. Notified vacancies are provisional and subject to variation at the discretion of the Director-cum-VC, SVIMS. Candidates may choose a test centre with three preferences, but AP Online Services reserves the right to allot any centre; requests for centre change will not be entertained. Mere participation in the exam or securing qualifying marks does not confer any right to selection or appointment; selection is provisional and subject to verification of original certificates, community/local status, experience, antecedents, and medical fitness. Submission of false certificates or information can lead to cancellation of candidature/appointment and criminal action. No TA/DA is paid for attending the written test or interview. Use of mobile phones (even switched off), calculators, smart watches, Bluetooth devices, or other electronic gadgets is strictly prohibited at the exam venue. All disputes are subject to the jurisdiction of the Court of Law, Andhra Pradesh.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 67 vacancies in total - 34 under Notification No.02/Para & Non Para/2026 (Paramedical and Non-Paramedical, open to AP State natives) and 33 under Notification No.03/Non Para/2026 (Non-Paramedical, open to Tirupati District natives only).

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application closes on 10 August 2026, up to 11:59 p.m. Applications open on 15 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. Who is eligible to apply for SVIMS Recruitment Notification No.02?
faq.answer: Candidates native to the State of Andhra Pradesh (post bifurcation) who profess the Hindu religion and meet the post-specific educational qualification and age criteria are eligible.

faq.question: Q4. Who is eligible to apply for SVIMS Recruitment Notification No.03?
faq.answer: Only candidates native to Tirupati District (post bifurcation of the districts) who profess the Hindu religion and meet the post-specific criteria are eligible.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 42 years of age as on 1 July 2026, with age relaxations of up to 5 years for SC/ST/BC (and EWS, under Notification 03) candidates, and 5 years for eligible SVIMS Adhoc/Outsourcing/Contract staff. The maximum age with all relaxations combined is 52 years.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is based on 100 marks: a 75-mark Computer Based Test, 15 marks for experience, and 10 marks for interview. Posts with fewer than 10 applicants are selected via a weightage method instead of a CBT. The Steno Cum Junior Assistant post additionally requires a separate 50-mark Skill Test.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Rs. 2,360/- for OC (and EWS, under Notification 03) and Rs. 1,770/- for BC/SC/ST candidates, inclusive of 18% GST. The Steno Cum Junior Assistant Skill Test carries an additional fee of Rs. 590/-.

faq.question: Q8. How can I apply online for SVIMS Tirupati Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online only, through the "Jobs" tab at svimstpt.ap.nic.in, by uploading the required documents and paying the fee via the payment gateway before the closing date.
